    4. Errors and fixes:
  • pptxgenjs 8-digit hex alpha colors: Caused warnings; fixed by using fill:{color:COLOR, transparency:N} instead of 8-digit hex

  • PowerPoint repair prompt: Caused by notesSlide references in rels; fixed by stripping them in _merge_final_pptx.py

  • 光纖通訊 framing issue: Initially suggested "有線拓撲" rename, but user correctly noted that even wired flight is impossible AND the succession mechanism is a separate question. Resolution: Drop wireless vs fiber distinction entirely; just use the two formations as the comparison axis


    • 5. Problem Solving:
  • 40% 攔阻率: Confirmed real data from _gnnonline_out.txt via _make_p4_charts.py. Definition: percentage of arrowhead formation scenarios where n_through == 0 (complete defense success). GNN=40%, RF=20%, Traditional=0%.

  • Q2 (CV→Kalman): Discovered no code change needed — KalmanCV (Kalman Filter with CV model) is already the traditional baseline in core/defense.py. Q2 becomes a slide text rename only: "傳統CV" → "傳統卡爾曼 CV" or "傳統卡爾曼濾波"

  • 光纖/Mesh contradiction: User realized Mesh topology eliminates single-leader vulnerability (making beheading pointless with limited missiles), so the wireless/fiber distinction doesn't work. Solution: Keep just the two formations, frame them around the two defense weaknesses they exploit

    Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\User\AppData\Roaming\Claude\local-agent-mode-sessions\skills-plugin\8e78da48-1d0e-4b26-8881-1482a84379f1\fc154731-bd80-4112-af84-ad512c485e24\skills\pptx

    PPTX Skill



    Quick Reference



    | Task | Guide |
    |------|-------|
    | Read/analyze content | extract-text presentation.pptx |
    | Edit or create from template | Read [editing.md](editing.md) |
    | Create from scratch | Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) |



    Reading Content



    # Text extraction, one ## Slide N section per slide
extract-text presentation.pptx


    Visual overview
    
python scripts/thumbnail.py presentation.pptx


    Raw XML
    
python scripts/office/unpack.py presentation.pptx unpacked/




    Editing Workflow



    Read [editing.md](editing.md) for full details.

    1. Analyze template with thumbnail.py
    2. Unpack → manipulate slides → edit content → clean → pack



    Creating from Scratch



    Read [pptxgenjs.md](pptxgenjs.md) for full details.

    Use when no template or reference presentation is available.



    Design Ideas



    Don't create boring slides. Plain bullets on a white background won't impress anyone. Consider ideas from this list for each slide.

    Before Starting



  • Pick a bold, content-informed color palette: The palette should feel designed for THIS topic. If swapping your colors into a completely different presentation would still "work," you haven't made specific enough choices.

  • Dominance over equality: One color should dominate (60-70% visual weight), with 1-2 supporting tones and one sharp accent. Never give all colors equal weight.

  • Dark/light contrast: Dark backgrounds for title + conclusion slides, light for content ("sandwich" structure). Or commit to dark throughout for a premium feel.

  • Commit to a visual motif: Pick ONE distinctive element and repeat it — rounded image frames, icons in colored circles. Carry it across every slide. Do not use a color bar or accent stripe as your motif (see Avoid list).


    • Color Palettes



    Choose colors that match your topic — don't default to generic blue. Use these palettes as inspiration:

    | Theme | Primary | Secondary | Accent |
    |-------|---------|-----------|--------|
    | Midnight Executive | 1E2761 (navy) | CADCFC (ice blue) | FFFFFF (white) |
    | Forest & Moss | 2C5F2D (forest) | 97BC62 (moss) | F5F5F5 (cream) |
    | Coral Energy | F96167 (coral) | F9E795 (gold) | 2F3C7E (navy) |
    | Warm Terracotta | B85042 (terracotta) | E7E8D1 (sand) | A7BEAE (sage) |
    | Ocean Gradient | 065A82 (deep blue) | 1C7293 (teal) | 21295C (midnight) |
    | Charcoal Minimal | 36454F (charcoal) | F2F2F2 (off-white) | 212121 (black) |
    | Teal Trust | 028090 (teal) | 00A896 (seafoam) | 02C39A (mint) |
    | Berry & Cream | 6D2E46 (berry) | A26769 (dusty rose) | ECE2D0 (cream) |
    | Sage Calm | 84B59F (sage) | 69A297 (eucalyptus) | 50808E (slate) |
    | Cherry Bold | 990011 (cherry) | FCF6F5 (off-white) | 2F3C7E (navy) |

    For Each Slide



    Every slide needs a visual element — image, chart, icon, or shape. Text-only slides are forgettable.

    Layout options:
  • Two-column (text left, illustration on right)

  • Icon + text rows (icon in colored circle, bold header, description below)

  • 2x2 or 2x3 grid (image on one side, grid of content blocks on other)

  • Half-bleed image (full left or right side) with content overlay


    • Data display:
  • Large stat callouts (big numbers 60-72pt with small labels below)

  • Comparison columns (before/after, pros/cons, side-by-side options)

  • Timeline or process flow (numbered steps, arrows)


    • Visual polish:
  • Icons in small colored circles next to section headers

  • Italic accent text for key stats or taglines


    • Typography



    Font names you write into the .pptx are rendered by the user's PowerPoint, not by this environment. Your visual QA renders via LibreOffice, which substitutes fonts it doesn't have — and for some fonts the substitute has different widths, so your QA preview can show text overflow (or fit) that the real deck won't have. To keep your QA trustworthy:

  • Safe fonts (render true-to-width in QA *and* ship with Office): Arial, Calibri, Cambria, Times New Roman, Courier New, Bookman Old Style, Century Schoolbook. Use these for body text and anything where fit matters.

  • Headers with personality at zero QA risk: pair a safe-list serif header (Cambria, Bookman Old Style, Century Schoolbook) with a safe-list sans body (Calibri or Arial). You get visual contrast without giving up reliable overflow checks.

  • If the user asks for a font outside the safe list (e.g. Georgia or Trebuchet MS): use it where the user asked, but size those containers with extra slack (~10%) and don't trust QA text-fit on those elements — the preview of that font is approximate. If the user hasn't specified, prefer safe-list fonts for body text.

  • QA-unreliable fonts (substitute has different widths — overflow checks can be wrong): Georgia, Trebuchet MS, Impact, Arial Black, Garamond, Consolas, Palatino Linotype. Calibri Light substitution varies by environment; treat as QA-unreliable. Fine for titles/accents with slack; don't trust QA text-fit on these.

  • Never default to Aptos — Office's post-2023 default has no metric-compatible substitute here *and* is missing from older Office installs, so it's unreliable on both ends.


    • | Element | Size |
    |---------|------|
    | Slide title | 36-44pt bold |
    | Section header | 20-24pt bold |
    | Body text | 14-16pt |
    | Captions | 10-12pt muted |

    Spacing



  • 0.5" minimum margins

  • 0.3-0.5" between content blocks

  • Leave breathing room—don't fill every inch


    • Avoid (Common Mistakes)



  • Don't repeat the same layout — vary columns, cards, and callouts across slides

  • Don't center body text — left-align paragraphs and lists; center only titles

  • Don't skimp on size contrast — titles need 36pt+ to stand out from 14-16pt body

  • Don't default to blue — pick colors that reflect the specific topic

  • Don't mix spacing randomly — choose 0.3" or 0.5" gaps and use consistently

  • Don't style one slide and leave the rest plain — commit fully or keep it simple throughout

  • Don't create text-only slides — add images, icons, charts, or visual elements; avoid plain title + bullets

  • Don't forget text box padding — when aligning lines or shapes with text edges, set margin: 0 on the text box or offset the shape to account for padding

  • Don't use low-contrast elements — icons AND text need strong contrast against the background; avoid light text on light backgrounds or dark text on dark backgrounds

  • NEVER use accent lines under titles — these are a hallmark of AI-generated slides; use whitespace or background color instead

  • NEVER add decorative color bars or accent stripes — this includes: header/footer bars spanning the slide width, vertical sidebar stripes down one edge of the slide, thin accent stripes along one edge of a card or content block, and "single-side borders" on rectangles. These read as AI-generated filler. If you want to set a card apart, use a subtle background tint, a drop shadow, or an icon — not an edge stripe.

  • Don't default to cream/beige backgrounds — when no background is specified, use white (FFFFFF) or the user's brand palette; avoid warm-neutral defaults like F5F5DC, FAF0E6, FAEBD7, FFF8E1

  • Don't ship text that overflows its shape — if text doesn't fit, reduce font size, split across slides, or enlarge the container; never leave content cut off or spilling past bounds




    • QA (Required)



    Your first render usually has a few real issues — overlaps, overflow, misalignment. Find and fix those, then stop. Don't keep iterating on minor coordinate nudges or chase a "perfect" render.

    Work, don't narrate: minimize prose between tool calls. Run the check, apply the fix, move on.

    Content QA



    extract-text output.pptx


    Check for missing content, typos, wrong order.

    When using templates, check for leftover placeholder text:

    extract-text output.pptx | grep -iE "\bx{3,}\b|lorem|ipsum|\bTODO|\[insert|this.*(page|slide).*layout"


    If grep returns results, fix them before declaring success.

    Visual QA



    ⚠️ USE SUBAGENTS — even for 2-3 slides. You've been staring at the code and will see what you expect, not what's there. Subagents have fresh eyes.

    Convert slides to images (see [Converting to Images](#converting-to-images)), then use this prompt:

    Visually inspect these slides for user-visible defects.

Look for:

  • Overlapping elements (text through shapes, lines through words, stacked elements)
    • 

  • Text overflow or cut off at edges/box boundaries
    • 

  • Source citations or footers colliding with content above
    • 

  • Elements too close (< 0.3" gaps) or cards/sections nearly touching
    • 

  • Uneven gaps (large empty area in one place, cramped in another)
    • 

  • Insufficient margin from slide edges (< 0.5")
    • 

  • Columns or similar elements not aligned consistently
    • 

  • Low-contrast text (e.g., light gray text on cream-colored background)
    • 

  • Template decoration mispositioned after text replacement — e.g., a title underline positioned for one line, but the replaced title wrapped to two
    • 

  • Low-contrast icons (e.g., dark icons on dark backgrounds without a contrasting circle)
    • 

  • Text boxes too narrow causing excessive wrapping
    • 

  • Leftover placeholder content
    • 

For each slide, list user-visible issues. Skip sub-pixel positioning and cosmetic nitpicks a viewer wouldn't notice.

Read and analyze these images — run ls -1 "$PWD"/slide-*.jpg and use the exact absolute paths it prints:
1. <absolute-path>/slide-N.jpg — (Expected: [brief description])
2. <absolute-path>/slide-N.jpg — (Expected: [brief description])
...


    Verification Loop



    1. Generate slides → Convert to images → Inspect
    2. Check text bounds first — for every text box, confirm the rendered text fits inside its shape. Overflow is the most common defect and is always user-visible. (Exception: for text in a QA-unreliable font per the Typography section, the preview is approximate — rely on the ~10% slack you added, not on the preview's apparent fit.)
    3. List any other issues found
    4. Fix issues
    5. Re-verify only the affected slides
    6. Stop after one fix-and-verify cycle unless a new *user-visible* defect appears (overlap, overflow, missing content). Do not loop on sub-pixel positioning, minor color tweaks, or issues a viewer wouldn't notice.



    Converting to Images



    Convert presentations to individual slide images for visual inspection:

    python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf output.pptx
rm -f slide-*.jpg
pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 output.pdf slide
ls -1 "$PWD"/slide-*.jpg


    Pass the absolute paths printed above directly to the view tool. The rm clears stale images from prior runs. pdftoppm zero-pads based on page count: slide-1.jpg for decks under 10 pages, slide-01.jpg for 10-99, slide-001.jpg for 100+.

    After fixes, rerun all four commands above — the PDF must be regenerated from the edited .pptx before pdftoppm can reflect your changes.



    Dependencies



  • pip install Pillow - thumbnail grids

  • npm install -g pptxgenjs - creating from scratch

  • LibreOffice (soffice) - PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via scripts/office/soffice.py)

  • Poppler (pdftoppm) - PDF to images

    This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

    Summary:
    1. Primary Request and Intent:
    The user is a thesis student presenting a UAV swarm attack/defense simulation project. This session has been iteratively refining UAV_完整簡報.pptx (PowerPoint). Completed tasks this session:
  • Q1: Remove all "seed 93" mentions from slides

  • Q2: Rename "CV"/"等速外插CV"/"傳統CV" → "卡爾曼 CV" throughout (no code change, slides only — KalmanCV is already implemented in code)

  • Q3: Remove GBM/LightGBM from all slides

  • Fixed 目錄 entry for 要點1 (was "陣型容錯機制" → "攻擊陣型 × 防空弱點")

  • Added chapter transition slides before each 要點 (大標1-5) + 感謝聆聽

  • Added 3 Claude AI usage slides (做了哪些事 / 工作流程 / Prompt示範)

  • Added Claude 安裝介紹 slide (如何使用 Claude | 安裝・應用・Claude Code 開發)

  • Synced merge pipeline to user's manual modifications

  • User declared current UAV_完整簡報.pptx (31 slides) as the working base for all future edits


    • Pending: Add 1 new slide showing actual Q&A example from this project ("我問了AI什麼？AI回答什麼？以這個實作為例")

    2. Key Technical Concepts:
  • pptxgenjs (Node.js): PPTX creation from scratch; card layouts, pill badges, step flows, bar charts

  • ZIP-level PPTX merge/insert: Direct manipulation of PPTX as ZIP; sldIdLst ordering, rId management, media renaming

  • _insert_at_pos.py: Reusable general-purpose insertion script. Inserts slides from a source PPTX into the base PPTX at a specific 1-indexed position. Avoids duplicate sldId via running_max_sid counter.

  • sldIdLst vs filename order: PowerPoint plays slides in sldIdLst order, NOT by slide filename. show_slides.py sorts by filename (wrong for verifying order); show_order.py reads sldIdLst (correct).

  • NODE_PATH: C:\Users\User\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules — must be set before node make_*.js

  • PYTHONUTF8=1: Must always be set for Python commands

  • Color palette: BG="0A0E14", PANEL="111820", PNL2="0d1a28", GOLD="FFD700", FG="E6EDF3", MUTED="8AA0B5", RED="FF5555", GREEN="39D353", BLUE="4488FF", CYAN="00E5FF", ORANGE="FF9900"


    • 3. Files and Code Sections:

  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\UAV_完整簡報.pptx — THE SOURCE OF TRUTH (31 slides). User declared "我要開始修改了 我希望你用這一版去改". All future changes go directly into this file via insertion scripts.


  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\_insert_at_pos.py — Reusable insertion script:

    •   BASE         = r"C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\UAV_完整簡報.pptx"
  INSERT_PPTX  = r"C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\UAV_claude_intro_slide.pptx"
  INSERT_AFTER = 1   # insert after slide N (1-indexed); new slide becomes N+1

    Uses running_max_sid to track unique sldId values. Change INSERT_PPTX and INSERT_AFTER to reuse for future insertions.

  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\make_claude_intro_slide.js — 1-slide PPTX about Claude installation/usage. 3-column card layout: ① 安裝 Claude Code, ② 三種應用方式, ③ Claude Code 開發方式. Bottom banner shows environment info.


  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\make_ai_process_slides.js — 3-slide Claude AI process PPTX: (A) 做了哪些事 — 6 category cards 3×2 grid; (B) 人機協作工作流程 — 5-step flow + loop banner; (C) 提示詞示範 & 心得 — 2 prompts left + 4 insight cards right.


  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\make_chapter_slides.js — 6-slide PPTX: 5 chapter transition slides (colored badge + large title + subtitle) + 感謝聆聽 (gold "感謝聆聽" + "Thanks for Listening" + divider).


  • C:\Users\User\Desktop\UAV\_merge_final_pptx.py — Updated MERGE_ORDER (for future full rebuilds only):

    •   MERGE_ORDER = [
      (r"UAV_intro_slides.pptx",      [0]),
      (r"UAV_ai_process_slides.pptx", None),

  • Scratchpad show_order.py (C:\Users\User\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\...\scratchpad\show_order.py) — reads slides in ACTUAL sldIdLst presentation order. Use this to verify insertions. show_slides.py sorts by filename and is WRONG for verifying order.


  • make_explanation_slides.js — Key changes this session:

    • - Line 185: subtitle removed "seed 93 基準場景"
    - Line 205: table header "卡爾曼 CV 防守" (was "傳統CV防守")
    - Line 398: "對比卡爾曼 CV（等速模型）的結構性差異"
    - Line 435: "相比卡爾曼 CV（卡爾曼濾波・等速模型）"
    - Line 471: bar chart label "卡爾曼 CV" (was "等速外插 CV")
    - Line 595: "多場景統計" (was "seed = 93")
    - Line 603: LightGBM entry deleted
    - Line 624: "卡爾曼 CV（不斬首）" (was "傳統CV（不斬首）")

  • make_p3_slides.js:

    • - Line 33: "傳統防空：卡爾曼 CV 預測" (was "傳統防空：等速外插預測（CV）")

  • make_intro_slides.js:

    • - Line 96 (目錄): title="攻擊陣型 × 防空弱點", sub="兩種陣型 · 注意力上限 × 彈藥上限 · 針對傳統防空設計"
    - Line 177: "卡爾曼 CV" label in slide 3
    - Line 324: Phase 3 description updated (removed GBM)
    - Line 364: scikit-learn only (removed LightGBM)
    - Line 328: "卡爾曼 CV vs AI 全整合"

    4. Errors and Fixes:
  • pptxgenjs MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Node can't find pptxgenjs without NODE_PATH. Fix: always set $env:NODE_PATH = "C:\Users\User\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules" before running node commands.

  • Inline Python in PowerShell: Multi-line Python -c string with | operators fails in PowerShell. Fix: always write Python to a .py file and run with python filename.py.

  • Duplicate sldId (all=286): _insert_ai_slides.py called max_sld_id(slst) in a loop without updating slst, so all new slides got the same id. Fix: use running_max_sid counter initialized before the loop, incremented each iteration.

  • Double-insertion bug: Ran _insert_ai_slides.py twice (once with bug, once fixed), each time on the already-modified PPTX, resulting in 33 slides. Fix: Rebuilt clean 27-slide base from source, then ran insertion once.

  • show_slides.py reads in wrong order: The scratchpad show_slides.py sorts by filename (slide1.xml, slide2.xml...) NOT by sldIdLst order. New slides get high filename numbers (slide31.xml) and appear at the end even if inserted at position 2. Fix: wrote show_order.py which reads sldIdLst and maps rIds to actual file content.


    • 5. Problem Solving:
  • Working on user's modified PPTX as base: After multiple rebuild cycles, user moved Claude slides to the front and declared current version as the working base. Strategy changed to direct ZIP-level insertion rather than rebuilding from JS source files.

  • Insertion position verification: show_order.py (sldIdLst-based) is the correct way to verify insertions; show_slides.py (filename-based) appears to show wrong order but is actually still useful for checking content.

    投影片內容是兩組對話氣泡格式：
  • 場景一（藍框）：問架構設計 → Claude 給出 5 個主類別骨架（Drone / Swarm / Interceptor / Radar / Simulator）

  • 場景二（橘框）：問 GNN 離線 87.1% vs 線上 40% 落差 → Claude 分析分布偏移 + 決策延遲兩根因

    我至今為止跟你的對話內容有辦法匯出給別人看嗎
    2026-06-27 14:50:57
    Claude
    幾種方式：

    1. 直接截圖/複製
    最簡單——直接截圖對話畫面，或框選文字複製貼到文件。

    2. claude.ai 網頁版的 Share 功能
    如果你是透過 [claude.ai](https://claude.ai) 開啟的對話，右上角通常有「Share」按鈕，可以產生一個公開連結讓別人瀏覽整段對話。

    3. 本機 JSONL 原始檔
    Claude Code 會把這個 session 的完整對話存在：
    C:\Users\User\.claude\projects\C--Users-User-Desktop---10-\
9de883ce-5c73-407a-8a95-c9136ea48b06.jsonl

    這是 JSON Lines 格式，內容包含所有訊息，但可讀性差（需要程式解析）。如果要分享給懂技術的人，可以直接傳這個檔。



    最實際的建議：
  • 給一般人看 → 截圖或複製貼到 Word/PDF

  • 給技術人員看 → 傳 .jsonl 檔

  • 要有互動式連結 → 用 claude.ai 網頁版的 Share 功能（但你目前用的是 Claude Code 桌面版，這個對話不一定有對應的網頁連結）
